audience: onchain traders, market makers, and DeFi builders who know what a central-limit order book is and are skeptical that one can work onchain
objective: get them to open the live demo exchange at clob.repo.box and place their first order
tone: confident, technically precise, a little swaggering — speaks to people who already understand markets, never condescending, no marketing fluff
docs:
Frontier is a full central-limit order book (CLOB) that lives entirely onchain — "the order book is back onchain." No operator, no off-chain matching engine, nothing to trust but the chain.

The problem it solves: onchain books historically died the moment they got useful — a taker crossing thousands of thin price levels paid a gas bill per level, so fine-grained books were unaffordable. Frontier removes that tax.

Core features:
- Thin ticks without a tax: production books use a 1 basis-point (0.01%) geometric tick curve; the demo uses $0.001 linear ticks to make it visible. The gas cost does not scale with how fine the grid is.
- Range orders (ladders, not single orders): quote a whole price range in one transaction — many limit orders across adjacent ticks, owned as one position. The core stores the ladder as endpoint deltas, so deposit cost is independent of range width. Fills become a contiguous prefix; you claim filled proceeds and cancel the unfilled remainder.
- Copy liquidity: mirror another quoting curve without running the same requote loop. It is additive — copied size sits beside active depth (up to 100% more), never hiding native resting liquidity.
- Frontier sweep: the frontier is the live edge of the book. A taker sweep moves that edge through resting liquidity in closed form instead of looping over every maker. In the benchmark a naive 5,000-level sweep costs 286.8M gas; Frontier settles the same shape at 214,805 gas — about a 1,335x compression.
- Your fills wait for you: settlement is lazy. When the market trades through your prices the proceeds are yours onchain, accruing, claimable whenever. Nothing expires, nothing needs a keeper — claim a month later if you like.
- Bots without custody: hand a bot the keys to your quotes, never your coins. The registry issues per-action, optionally-expiring grants (e.g. grant exactly `requote` and nothing else); EIP-712 permits allow gasless granting. Payouts only ever go to the owner.
- Books that do things: v4-style hooks turn a book into its own TWAP oracle, a gated market, a circuit-breaker venue, or a rewards program — implemented and tested, not roadmap.

Chains & cost: operations are benchmarked as real transactions and priced in dollars on Ethereum, Base, and Gnosis. On Gnosis or Base, essentially everything costs a few tens of cents; Ethereum mainnet is more expensive but still practical. Taker cost is driven by the number of distinct maker order endpoints crossed (~10–13k gas marginal each), not by the number of thin levels between them.

Custody: non-custodial. Owners keep their coins; delegated authorization is scoped and never transfers custody.

Try it: the live demo exchange is at clob.repo.box — a real market on a devnet. It creates a funded demo wallet in your browser on first load; hit Faucet to mint test WETH + USDC; demo market-maker bots quote around the live Coinbase ETH price and the book settles every fill onchain. There is also a 30-seconds-to-your-first-fill walkthrough in the demo guide.
